Uplink

Computer-Assisted Reporting Newsletter, 1990–2007

Browse Topics Search

Here's a look at our hardware & software

September 1994
Technology

A newsroom's technology inventory from the early 1990s detailing computer hardware, operating systems, and software applications including PCs, Unix systems, and various productivity tools for journalism work.

PCs: Four 486 IBM compatibles, 8mb RAM, color monitors, +300mb hard drives, three CD readers. PC operating systems: DOS, Windows and OS/2. Mini/mainframe: Newsroom has access to an HP9000 with Unix operating system and 9-track tape drive. Printers: Lasers, dots and access to a CalComp DesignMate. Spreadsheet: Quattro Pro for Windows. Database: FoxPro, dBase IV. Text & word processing: Word for Windows, Xywrite.

Mapping & GIS: Atlas GIS/DOS 21, Atlas GIS Windows 2.0. Statistical analysis: SAS, SPSSPC/DOS, SPSS/Windows.
